definitions.subtitle: ={{$parameter["operation"] + ": " + $parameter["resource"]}}
definitions.description: Interact with EVM blockchains via 1Shot API

options.name: Set Automatically
options.description: Automatically set based on resource and operation

options.name: Set Manually
options.description: Manually set the description

properties.name: toolDescription
properties.default: Interact with EVM blockchains via 1Shot API
properties.description: Explain to the LLM what this tool does, a good, specific description would allow LLMs to produce expected results much more often

options.name: List Chains
options.description: List chains supported by 1Shot API

properties.name: page
properties.description: Enter the page number to get. This starts at 1.

properties.name: pageSize
properties.description: Enter the size of the page to get

options.name: Create
options.description: Create a new contract event definition

options.name: Delete
options.description: Delete a contract event definition

options.name: Get
options.description: Get a single contract event definition

options.name: List
options.description: List all contract event definitions

options.name: Search
options.description: Search for contract event logs on the blockchain

options.name: Update
options.description: Update an existing contract event definition

properties.name: contractEventId
properties.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

properties.name: name
properties.description: Filter contract events by name

properties.name: contractAddress
properties.description: Filter contract events by contract address

properties.name: eventName
properties.description: Filter contract events by event name

properties.name: chainId

properties.name: chainId
properties.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

properties.name: contractAddress
properties.description: Enter the address of the contract to monitor for events

properties.name: name
properties.description: A human-readable name for this event definition

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of what this event represents

properties.name: eventName
properties.description: The exact name of the event as defined in the contract ABI

properties.name: name
properties.description: Updated name for the event definition

properties.name: description
properties.description: Updated description for the event definition

properties.name: startBlock
properties.description: Starting block number to search from

properties.name: endBlock
properties.description: Ending block number to search to

properties.name: topics
properties.description: Filter by indexed event parameters. Add topic name-value pairs to filter the search results.

values.name: name
values.description: The name of the indexed topic parameter (e.g., "from", "to", "tokenId")

properties.name: page
properties.description: Enter the page number to get. This starts at 1.

properties.name: pageSize
properties.description: Enter the size of the page to get

options.name: Assure Contract Methods From Prompt
options.description: This will make sure you have a set of Contract Methods ready to use based on your chosen Prompt

options.name: Encode
options.description: Encode a contract method call. This produces the calldata for the contract method, which is sometimes required for other contract methods.
options.action: Encode a smart contract method call

options.name: Estimate
options.description: Get an estimate of the gas cost of a contract method
options.action: Estimate the gas cost of a smart contract method

options.name: Execute
options.description: Execute a smart contract method on the blockchain

options.name: Execute as Delegator
options.description: Execute a smart contract method on the blockchain using a stored ERC-7710 delegation
options.action: Execute a smart contract method as delegator

options.name: Execute Batch
options.description: Execute multiple smart contract methods in a single transaction

options.name: Execute Batch as Delegator
options.description: Execute multiple smart contract methods as a delegator in a single transaction
options.action: Execute multiple smart contract methods as delegator

options.name: Get
options.description: Get a single smart contract method

options.name: List
options.description: List all smart contract methods

options.name: Read
options.description: Read data from a smart contract view or pure function
options.action: Read data from a smart contract function

options.name: Simulate
options.description: Simulate a smart contract method call and see if it would succeed with the current state of the chain
options.action: Simulate a smart contract method call

properties.name: contractMethodId
properties.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

properties.name: contractMethodId
properties.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

values.name: contractMethodId
values.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

values.name: params
values.description: The parameters to pass to the Contract Method. Enter a JSON object (e.g., {"to": "0x3e6a2f0CBA03d293B54c9fCF354948903007a798", "amount": "10000"}).

options.name: contractAddress
options.description: Set this to override the default contract address for this method execution

options.name: value
options.description: The amount of native token to send along with the Contract Method

values.name: contractMethodId
values.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

values.name: params
values.description: The parameters to pass to the Contract Method. Enter a JSON object (e.g., {"to": "0x3e6a2f0CBA03d293B54c9fCF354948903007a798", "amount": "10000"}).

values.name: delegatorAddress
values.description: The address of the delegator on whose behalf the transaction will be executed. Note: You can only use one of delegatorAddress, delegationId, or delegationData.

values.name: delegationId
values.description: The ID of a specific delegation to use for this transaction. This is the best way to ensure that 1Shot API uses the correct delegation. Note: You can only use one of delegatorAddress, delegationId, or delegationData.

values.name: delegationData
values.description: A delegation object to use for the transaction. This is treated as a one-time use delegation and not stored in 1Shot API for reuse. Enter a JSON object (e.g., {"authority":"0xffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffff","caveats":[],"delegate":"0x74847c451c3745fcdf4fd447a9fa5eef8a6791a9","delegator":"0x3e6a2f0CBA03d293B54c9fCF354948903007a798","salt":"0x","signature":"0x000007dc509f5f440bd07e2a51cb8dd135f170bc8886ccf24c35652d256d4f06501537e663fbb3b6e51ca640ad4791fb605ba7434d4d8a0a0a24bfd4d841e88f1c"}). Note: You can only use one of delegatorAddress, delegationId, or delegationData.

options.name: contractAddress
options.description: Set this to override the default contract address for this method execution

options.name: value
options.description: The amount of native token to send along with the Contract Method

properties.name: params
properties.description: The parameters to pass to the Contract Method. Enter a JSON object (e.g., {"to": "0x3e6a2f0CBA03d293B54c9fCF354948903007a798", "amount": "10000"}).

properties.displayName: You must use one of either delegatorAddress, delegationId, or delegationData.
properties.name: delegationInfoMessage
delegatorWalletAddress
properties.description: The address of the delegator wallet to use for the contract method. Note: You can only use one of delegatorAddress, delegationId, or delegationData.

properties.name: delegationId
properties.description: The ID of a specific delegation to use for this transaction. This is the best way to ensure that 1Shot API uses the correct delegation. Note: You can only use one of delegatorAddress, delegationId, or delegationData.

properties.name: delegationData
properties.description: A delegation object to use for the transaction. This is treated as a one-time use delegation and not stored in 1Shot API for reuse. Enter a JSON object (e.g., {"authority":"0xffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffff","caveats":[],"delegate":"0x74847c451c3745fcdf4fd447a9fa5eef8a6791a9","delegator":"0x3e6a2f0CBA03d293B54c9fCF354948903007a798","salt":"0x","signature":"0x000007dc509f5f440bd07e2a51cb8dd135f170bc8886ccf24c35652d256d4f06501537e663fbb3b6e51ca640ad4791fb605ba7434d4d8a0a0a24bfd4d841e88f1c"}). Note: You can only use one of delegatorAddress, delegationId, or delegationData.

options.description: The ID of the Wallet to use for this Contract Method

options.description: Optional text to include with the Transaction after the Contract Method is executed

options.name: contractAddress
options.description: Set this to override the default contract address for this method execution

options.name: value
options.description: The amount of native token to send along with the Contract Method

options.name: authorizationList
options.description: List of ERC-7702 authorizations for the Contract Method

options.name: gasLimit
options.description: The gas limit to use for the Contract Method. The higher of either this value or the estimated gas will be used.

options.name: authorizationList
options.description: List of ERC-7702 authorizations for the Contract Method

options.name: atomic
options.description: Whether or not all the transactions in the batch must succeed, or the entire batch is rolled back

options.name: memo
options.description: Optional text to include with the Transaction after the batch is executed

options.name: authorizationList
options.description: List of ERC-7702 authorizations for the batch execution

options.name: gasLimit
options.description: The gas limit to use for the batch execution. The higher of either this value or the estimated gas will be used.

options.name: X402 HTTP Request
options.description: Make an HTTP request to an x402-enabled API endpoint. Any fee will be paid using a 1Shot Wallet.

properties.name: walletId
properties.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

properties.name: method
properties.description: The request method to use

properties.name: url
properties.description: The URL to make the request to

options.name: Predefined Credential Type
options.description: We've already implemented auth for many services so that you don't have to set it up manually

options.name: Generic Credential Type
options.description: Fully customizable. Choose between basic, header, OAuth2, etc.

properties.displayName: Make sure you have specified the scope(s) for the Service Account in the credential
properties.name: googleApiWarning

properties.displayName: Provide certificates in node's 'Credential for SSL Certificates' parameter
properties.name: provideSslCertificatesNotice
sendQuery
properties.description: Whether the request has query params or not

properties.name: sendHeaders
properties.description: Whether the request has headers or not

properties.name: sendBody
properties.description: Whether the request has a body or not

properties.name: contentType
properties.description: Content-Type to use to send body parameters

properties.name: specifyBody
properties.description: The body can be specified using explicit fields (keypair) or using a JavaScript object (json)

values.name: name
values.description: ID of the field to set. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

values.name: value
values.description: Value of the field to set

values.name: name
values.description: ID of the field to set. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

values.name: value
values.description: Value of the field to set

values.name: inputDataFieldName
values.description: The name of the incoming field containing the binary file data to be processed

values.name: name
values.description: ID of the field to set. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

values.name: value
values.description: Value of the field to set

properties.name: inputDataFieldName
properties.description: The name of the incoming field containing the binary file data to be processed

values.name: batchSize
values.description: Input will be split in batches to throttle requests. -1 for disabled. 0 will be treated as 1.

values.name: batchInterval
values.description: Time (in milliseconds) between each batch of requests. 0 for disabled.

options.name: allowUnauthorizedCerts
options.description: Whether to download the response even if SSL certificate validation is not possible

options.name: No Brackets
options.description: e.g. foo=bar&foo=qux

options.name: Brackets Only
options.description: e.g. foo[]=bar&foo[]=qux

options.name: Brackets with Indices
options.description: e.g. foo[0]=bar&foo[1]=qux

options.name: lowercaseHeaders
options.description: Whether to lowercase header names

values.name: followRedirects
values.description: Whether to follow all redirects

values.name: maxRedirects
values.description: Max number of redirects to follow

values.name: followRedirects
values.description: Whether to follow all redirects

values.name: maxRedirects
values.description: Max number of redirects to follow

values.name: fullResponse
values.description: Whether to return the full response (headers and response status code) data instead of only the body

values.name: neverError
values.description: Whether to succeeds also when status code is not 2xx

values.name: responseFormat
values.description: The format in which the data gets returned from the URL

values.name: outputPropertyName
values.description: Name of the binary property to which to write the data of the read file

options.name: timeout
options.description: Time in ms to wait for the server to send response headers (and start the response body) before aborting the request

properties.name: optimizeResponse
properties.description: Whether the optimize the tool response to reduce amount of data passed to the LLM that could lead to better result and reduce cost

properties.name: dataField
properties.description: Specify the name of the field in the response containing the data
properties.hint: leave blank to use whole response

properties.name: fieldsToInclude
properties.description: What fields response object should include

options.name: All
options.description: Include all fields

options.name: Selected
options.description: Include only fields specified below

options.name: Except
options.description: Exclude fields specified below

properties.name: fields
properties.description: Comma-separated list of the field names. Supports dot notation. You can drag the selected fields from the input panel.

properties.name: cssSelector
properties.description: Select specific element(e.g. body) or multiple elements(e.g. div) of chosen type in the response HTML.

properties.name: onlyContent
properties.description: Whether to return only content of html elements, stripping html tags and attributes
properties.hint: Uses less tokens and may be easier for model to understand

properties.name: elementsToOmit
properties.description: Comma-separated list of selectors that would be excluded when extracting content

properties.displayName: You can view the raw requests this node makes in your browser's developer console
properties.name: infoMessage

definitions.name: n8n-nodes-1shot.oneShotTool
